mnemonics: CLOCKs Can't Time Travel (Causality, Lamport & Logical Clocks, Clocks for Ordering, Time Synchronization, TrueTime)
keyPoints: Logical clocks (like Lamport clocks) order events based on causality, not absolute time.
Physical clock synchronization (e.g., NTP) is necessary but imperfect for coordinating across machines.
A global state is a snapshot of the entire distributed system, but capturing it is challenging due to lack of a single global time.
commonMistakes: Assuming that because event A happened before event B in real time, a process will always see them in that order.
Believing that perfectly synchronized physical clocks are possible or necessary for all distributed algorithms.
Thinking a global snapshot is taken at a single instant in time, rather than being constructed from individual process states collected over an interval.
